item.title: City grandee Ian Cheshire favourite to be named Ofcom chair
item.description: Former Kingfisher boss vying for key internet and broadcast regulation role with politicians Margaret Hodge and Jeremy Wright

item.title: Microsoft launches ‘mid-class’ AI model as compute limits bite
item.description: Tech giant’s AI chief says it will have the resources to build frontier systems later this year

item.title: Tesla reports weaker than expected 6% rise in global deliveries
item.description: First-quarter increase comes as Chinese rival BYD’s electric vehicle sales fell 25% amid intensifying competition

item.title: UK, Italy and Japan sign first contract with industry for fighter jet project
item.description: Funding limited to three months while Britain finalises delayed defence investment plan

item.title: Blue Owl struck by $5.4bn of redemption requests
item.description: Private credit firm caps withdrawals after investors attempted to pull more than 40% from one fund

item.description: US authorities warned the banks of potential security threats

item.description: yikes

item.title: Will AI make it harder for non-graduates to climb the jobs ladder?
item.description: Gateway roles to white-collar work appear particularly exposed to disruption

item.title: Italy fines Revolut over misleading ‘0% commission’ trading adverts
item.description: Competition watchdog singles out advertisements suggesting customers could trade with no commission

item.title: Can UK house prices survive another economic shock?
item.description: The housing market looks to have made it through the crises of the 2020s unscathed — but they have weakened it significantly

item.title: A $750mn solar fund provides lifeline for capital-starved US projects
item.description: LA investment firm Forest Road will focus on smaller projects struggling after Trump’s ‘Big Beautiful Bill’

item.title: Nick Candy sells Chelsea mansion for more than £275mn
item.description: Deal for Providence House in central London marks capital’s most expensive house sale

item.title: Much ado about marshmallows
item.description: Good news, it’s s’more VAT nonsense

item.title: ‘End of an era’ as ECB’s emergency pandemic stimulus unwinds
item.description: Nearly €3tn of excess liquidity will have drained from financial system by 2027, according to ECB estimates

item.title: The UK’s unpleasant energy exposure
item.description: The vulnerability stems from history and geography rather than failed policies

item.description: Decision is boost for Big Four firm seeking to repair reputation after series of scandals

item.title: Air France-KLM kicks off bidding race for Portugal’s TAP
item.description: Franco-Dutch group first to submit non-binding offer for up to 49.9% of carrier

item.description: Distressed-debt funds see a potential bonanza in the downturn in private credit

item.title: World’s top energy traders wrongfooted in early days of Iran war
item.description: Oil traders that normally thrive on volatility caught out by scale of crisis as energy shock unfurled

item.title: From Lockheed to start-ups, arms makers jostle for Iran war orders
item.description: Middle East conflict holds out promise to bring another windfall for defence groups as governments restock arsenals

item.title: Gulf states consider new pipelines to avoid Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Conflict prompts countries to revisit plans that replicate Saudi Arabia’s East-West pipeline despite huge cost and complexity

item.title: Poolside hunts data centre partners after CoreWeave deal falls through
item.description: AI start-up held talks with Google and other cloud providers in bid to revive ambitious 2-gigawatt Texas project

item.title: Ofwat poised to waive Thames Water fines to 2030 under deal

item.title: Car park collapse is a warning to leaseback-loving retailers
item.description: UK supermarkets that sell and then rent back their property should take note of NCP’s failure
